planning_gate.spec_delta: M19 tests a full-rollout terminal stand objective over multi-step CoM/ZMP trajectory plans rather than another hand-picked parameter sweep.

perspectives.product: directly targets the 7cm recoverable gate that blocks a visible G1 squat
perspectives.architecture: keeps exp67 qfrc WBC selector as actuator layer and adds a terminal objective over the plan horizon
perspectives.security: local MuJoCo/JAX run only; no credentials or external writes
perspectives.qa: raw JSON per candidate plus sorted terminal objective summary
perspectives.skeptic: still a discrete black-box optimizer, not a real-time continuous MPC solver

dod: each candidate is evaluated by native MuJoCo rollout
summary reports terminal stand score, recoverable 7cm gate, and visible 8cm gate

sources.note: G1 is advertised with large joint movement angle space and imitation/RL-driven motion.
G1 developer guide describes six degrees of freedom per leg and waist DOF.
Humanoid squat can be formulated as TP-MPC trajectory optimization plus WBC tracking.
Real-time whole-body MPC uses ZMP/contact constraints over a horizon for bipedal robots.
ZMP/CoM framing connects humanoid balance to support-constrained CoM trajectory generation.
